# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - '**/*'

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: write
  deployments: write
  repository_dispatches: write
  secrets: read
  pull-requests: read
  issues: read
  check-suites: read
  pages: read
  workflows: read
  single-file: write

jobs:
  build-and-publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Build and publish packages
        run: |
          npm install
          npm run build
          npm publish

      - name: Create release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}
          TAG_NAME: ${{ github.sha }}
          DRAFT: false
          PRERELEASE: true

      - name: Update documentation
        run: |
          npm install
          npm run build-docs
